source_set.title: A harness for every task: dynamic workflows in Claude Code
Harness design for long-running application development
Lessons from building Claude Code: how we use skills
Lessons from building Claude Code: prompt caching is everything
Using Claude Code: the unreasonable effectiveness of HTML

adopted_principles.summary: Workflow design choices must point to official sources and adopted principles.
Dynamic task workflows guide execution, while the release harness remains the final gate.
Root instructions stay concise and link to durable docs, specs, and scripts.
Repos should expose state, logs, metrics, traces, UI feedback, and run commands to agents.
Long work needs explicit task state, blockers, evidence, and resumable handoffs.
Separate work execution from evaluation, review, and trajectory criticism.
Stable prefixes, stable tool surfaces, append-only updates, and compaction hygiene protect long workflows.
Repeated workflows should become focused skills with concise triggers and lazily loaded references or scripts.
Untrusted readers, privileged actors, sandbox boundaries, and hook gates stay separate.
Repeated agent failures and repo drift should become mechanical checks or cleanup loops.
Dense review artifacts can help humans and verifier agents inspect complex outputs.

workflow_classes.name: Focused Change
workflow_classes.purpose: Use the default release harness for small, well-scoped edits.
use_when: The task fits in one context window.
No API, runtime, persistence, provider, secret, task, or event semantics change.
Focused verification and release verification are sufficient.

context_strategy.session_boundary: continue only while the same task context is still load-bearing
context_strategy.context_rot: start a fresh session for genuinely new tasks
context_strategy.cache_policy: keep project instructions and tool surface stable
context_strategy.subagent_policy: avoid subagents unless verification output would be noisy

rubric: No unrelated files are changed.
Focused tests and release gate pass.
Docs or comments match final behavior.

stop_conditions: Focused verification passes.
Release harness passes or a blocker is reported.
human_escalation: Approval-required paths are touched.
A behavior change is discovered.

workflow_classes.name: Spec-first Feature
workflow_classes.purpose: Turn requests into spec-backed implementation, review, and release evidence.
use_when: The task changes API, runtime, event, task, config, provider, secret, DB, or persistence behavior.
Acceptance criteria need to be made executable.
The work must survive future AI-tool handoffs.

context_strategy.session_boundary: write or update the spec before implementation and start fresh if the task changes
context_strategy.context_rot: compact with explicit spec ids, excluded approaches, and remaining acceptance criteria
context_strategy.cache_policy: keep AGENTS and tool definitions stable; put status changes in messages or artifacts
context_strategy.subagent_policy: delegate review, source comparison, or noisy verification to child contexts

tool_policy.surface: scripts, tests, repo search, and release harness are primary tools

rubric: Spec IDs and workflow class are present.
Implementation follows the spec rather than raw chat.
Review finds no untested contract, auth, event, provider, secret, or persistence regression.

stop_conditions: Spec, implementation plan, code, review, and harness evidence align.
Unresolved product or operational gaps are escalated instead of hidden in code.
human_escalation: Public API, runtime orchestration, provider limits, secrets, persistence, or approval-required files change.
A spec-contract exemption would be required.

workflow_classes.name: Wide Refactor
workflow_classes.purpose: Coordinate broad mechanical or semantic refactors without losing coverage.
use_when: Many call sites, routes, tasks, runtime modules, or docs must change.
Independent slices can be reviewed separately.
One context window would hide partial completion.

context_strategy.session_boundary: one orchestrator context plus clean worker contexts per slice
context_strategy.context_rot: keep only slice summaries, conflicts, and unresolved blockers in the parent
context_strategy.cache_policy: do not mutate tool definitions while slices are running
context_strategy.subagent_policy: use one subagent per slice and one verifier per slice when risk is high

tool_policy.surface: repo search, tests, worktree operations, and release harness

rubric: Every slice has focused verification.
Synthesis merges only reviewed changes.
No stale names, partial migrations, or skipped call sites remain.

stop_conditions: All slices are complete or explicitly deferred.
Synthesis and adversarial review find no stale references.
Release harness passes.
human_escalation: The refactor changes behavior not covered by the spec.
Merge conflicts cross module ownership boundaries.

workflow_classes.name: Deep Verification
workflow_classes.purpose: Verify technical, release, or architecture claims with independent checks.
use_when: A spec, architecture note, release note, or report makes factual claims.
A wrong claim would create implementation or operational risk.
Source quality and traceability matter.

context_strategy.session_boundary: separate claim extraction, source lookup, and final synthesis
context_strategy.context_rot: drop raw source noise after extracting cited findings
context_strategy.cache_policy: keep source tools stable and pass fresh evidence in messages
context_strategy.subagent_policy: use one subagent per claim or source family when coverage matters

tool_policy.surface: source search, repo search, bounded fetchers, and evidence tables

rubric: Every material claim maps to evidence.
Source quality is checked separately from claim extraction.
Unverified claims are removed or marked as assumptions.

stop_conditions: No unchecked material claims remain.
Low-quality or missing sources are escalated.
Release harness passes when code changed.
human_escalation: Required evidence is unavailable.
The workflow cannot distinguish evidence from inference.

workflow_classes.name: Research Synthesis
workflow_classes.purpose: Fan out research, source fetching, source criticism, and synthesis into a cited deliverable.
use_when: The task requires broad evidence collection.
Multiple sources, code paths, logs, or docs need independent exploration.
The output must separate findings, assumptions, and uncertainty.

context_strategy.session_boundary: separate source collection, source criticism, and final synthesis
context_strategy.context_rot: carry forward only cited findings and unresolved contradictions
context_strategy.cache_policy: do not rewrite static instructions during source collection
context_strategy.subagent_policy: use subagents for independent source families or code areas

tool_policy.surface: web, repo, file system, data, and monitoring search through progressive disclosure

rubric: Research agents collect source-backed findings.
Verifier agents challenge source quality and unsupported inference.
Synthesis preserves uncertainty and cites evidence.

stop_conditions: Coverage is sufficient for the stated research question.
No high-impact claim lacks evidence.
Contradictory evidence is surfaced rather than averaged away.
human_escalation: Research scope is ambiguous enough to change the answer.
Freshness requirements cannot be met.

workflow_classes.name: Security Review
workflow_classes.purpose: Separate finding discovery, exploitability analysis, validation, and remediation review.
use_when: The task asks for security review, threat modeling, exploit validation, or fix review.
Findings need independent confirmation.
Untrusted inputs, logs, tickets, or external content may be inspected.

context_strategy.session_boundary: separate untrusted evidence readers from privileged fixers
context_strategy.context_rot: carry only validated findings and reproduction steps into remediation
context_strategy.cache_policy: keep security runbooks and tool stubs stable
context_strategy.subagent_policy: use independent discovery and validation subagents

tool_policy.surface: security scan, repo search, tests, and bounded log fetchers

rubric: Findings include an attack path and affected asset.
A separate validator confirms exploitability and severity.
Fixes are reviewed for bypasses and regressions.

stop_conditions: No open high-confidence findings remain untriaged.
Validated fixes pass focused tests and release harness.
Unvalidated findings are clearly marked.
human_escalation: A production secret, customer data, or active exploit is suspected.
A fix touches auth, provider secrets, runtime orchestration, or approval-required paths.

workflow_classes.name: Incident Triage
workflow_classes.purpose: Classify, dedupe, investigate, and escalate operational incidents without mixing untrusted evidence and privileged action.
use_when: The task involves logs, tickets, incidents, alerts, or recurring root causes.
The workflow may need to repeat until no new errors appear.
Some evidence comes from production or external systems.

context_strategy.session_boundary: separate log readers, hypothesis generators, and acting agents
context_strategy.context_rot: keep hypotheses, evidence, and disconfirming facts; drop raw log noise
context_strategy.cache_policy: keep runbook/tool stubs stable and pass fresh incidents as messages
context_strategy.subagent_policy: fan out by evidence type or incident cluster

tool_policy.surface: runbooks, bounded log queries, issue tracker, repo search

rubric: Classifiers separate duplicate, known, and novel incidents.
Investigators cite bounded logs or tickets.
Acting agents only change tracked issues or code after review.

stop_conditions: No new unique incident class appears in the bounded input.
Root-cause hypotheses have supporting and disconfirming evidence.
Escalations name the owner and next action.
human_escalation: Production credentials, raw customer data, or broad log access would be needed.
The workflow proposes write actions outside tracked code or issue systems.

workflow_classes.name: Exploration Tournament
workflow_classes.purpose: Explore open-ended alternatives, then select winners through a rubric-driven tournament.
use_when: The task asks for naming, design direction, API shape, architecture alternatives, or strategy options.
Multiple plausible answers should compete.
A rubric can evaluate pairwise choices more reliably than absolute scores.

context_strategy.session_boundary: separate generation from judging and keep the rubric stable
context_strategy.context_rot: carry only finalist summaries, tradeoffs, and rejected assumptions
context_strategy.cache_policy: do not change judging tools mid-tournament
context_strategy.subagent_policy: use one agent per candidate or pairwise comparison when quality matters

tool_policy.surface: rubrics, comparison matrices, optional HTML review artifacts

rubric: The rubric is explicit before judging starts.
Candidates are meaningfully distinct.
Winning options include tradeoffs and rejected alternatives.

stop_conditions: The judge or human rubric has selected a bounded shortlist.
No duplicate or dominated candidate remains in the shortlist.
human_escalation: The rubric requires product, brand, legal, or business-owner judgment.
The chosen option would change public contracts or runtime behavior.

workflow_classes.name: Long-run Task Graph
workflow_classes.purpose: Coordinate complex work across sessions, subagents, dependencies, blockers, and resumptions.
use_when: The work spans multiple sessions, subagents, or context windows.
Tasks have dependencies, blockers, or owners.
Progress must be resumable without relying on chat memory.

context_strategy.session_boundary: new sessions may join the same durable task list
context_strategy.context_rot: resume from task metadata, blockers, and evidence instead of full chat history
context_strategy.cache_policy: keep the task schema stable and record updates as task metadata
context_strategy.subagent_policy: subagents update assigned tasks and return only status, blockers, and evidence

tool_policy.surface: task metadata files, dependency graph, focused verifiers, release harness

rubric: Every task has state, owner, dependencies, blocker status, and evidence.
No task is marked complete without verification.
Resume state is sufficient for a new session to continue.

stop_conditions: All required tasks are complete or explicitly blocked.
Dependency blockers are resolved or escalated.
Release evidence covers completed tasks.
human_escalation: A blocker requires product ownership.
A dependency crosses repository or permission boundaries.

workflow_classes.name: Runtime Legibility
workflow_classes.purpose: Make the running system observable, controllable, and verifiable by agents and humans.
use_when: The change affects local startup, dev environments, UI feedback, observability, logs, metrics, traces, or smoke tests.
Agents need to inspect the app while it is running.
A project lacks deterministic commands for booting, probing, or debugging.

rubric: An agent can discover how to start and probe the system.
Logs, metrics, traces, or UI feedback identify failures without private knowledge.
Runtime evidence is captured under .artifacts/ when relevant.

stop_conditions: Documented run and smoke commands work or blockers are explicit.
Runtime signals expose enough context to diagnose the changed path.
Release harness passes.
human_escalation: Production credentials, private hosts, or unsafe network access are needed.
The runtime contract would become cloud-provider-specific.

context_strategy.session_boundary: keep run, observe, probe, and verify steps in one traceable task context
context_strategy.context_rot: carry only startup commands, observed signals, blockers, and evidence paths forward
context_strategy.cache_policy: keep runbooks and tool surfaces stable; pass environment-specific status as messages or artifacts
context_strategy.subagent_policy: use separate smoke or log readers when runtime output is noisy

tool_policy.surface: run commands, smoke scripts, logs, metrics, traces, screenshots, and release harness

workflow_classes.name: Eval Improvement Loop
workflow_classes.purpose: Convert repeated agent, prompt, workflow, or review failures into durable checks.
use_when: An agent repeatedly makes the same class of mistake.
A prompt, skill, hook, or workflow needs measurable improvement.
A previous review or release escaped a defect that should become a regression check.

rubric: The original failure is captured as an example or regression case.
The new check fails before the fix and passes after the fix when feasible.
Trajectory review confirms the process improvement, not only the final diff.

stop_conditions: The regression check, eval, hook, or script covers the failure class.
Verifier rerun finds no remaining high-impact escape.
Release harness passes.
human_escalation: The failure example includes secrets or sensitive production data.
The improvement would weaken an existing gate.

context_strategy.session_boundary: separate failure example capture, regression design, fix, and verifier rerun
context_strategy.context_rot: preserve the failure class, minimal repro, changed gate, and verification output
context_strategy.cache_policy: keep prompts, hooks, scripts, and tool surfaces stable while the eval loop runs
context_strategy.subagent_policy: use verifier contexts to criticize the trajectory and regression coverage

tool_policy.surface: evals, scripts, focused tests, trajectory notes, review rubrics, and release harness

workflow_classes.name: Interactive Artifact
workflow_classes.purpose: Use HTML or playground-style artifacts when humans need to inspect, compare, tune, or export structured decisions.
use_when: A markdown plan or report would be too dense to review reliably.
The human needs side-by-side comparison, visual feedback, or interactive editing.
The output should export a prompt, JSON, diff, or markdown back into the workflow.

context_strategy.session_boundary: treat the artifact as the human review surface and export only decisions back
context_strategy.context_rot: avoid carrying full artifact internals after export
context_strategy.cache_policy: build artifacts from stable templates or assets when repeated
context_strategy.subagent_policy: use visual or review subagents for complex artifacts

tool_policy.surface: HTML files, screenshots, copy/export buttons, optional browser verification

rubric: The artifact makes the decision easier to inspect than markdown.
The artifact has a clear export path back to code, prompt, JSON, diff, or markdown.
Visual or interactive behavior is verified when it affects the decision.

stop_conditions: The human-visible artifact is generated and inspectable.
The selected settings or decisions are exported in a reusable format.
Any code changes still pass release harness.
human_escalation: The artifact makes product, design, brand, or business tradeoffs that need owner approval.
The export would directly change production config or secrets.

workflow_classes.name: Skill Evolution
workflow_classes.purpose: Turn repeated corrections, runbooks, templates, scripts, and gotchas into reusable skills or project guidance.
use_when: The same correction, workflow, or setup question recurs.
A repo would benefit from reusable scripts, references, assets, gotchas, or hooks.
A skill or instruction needs evaluation before sharing.

context_strategy.session_boundary: mine prior evidence in a separate context and edit guidance in a clean context
context_strategy.context_rot: distill only rules that would have prevented real mistakes
context_strategy.cache_policy: keep descriptions concise because they enter skill listings
context_strategy.subagent_policy: use verifier subagents to reject over-specific or low-signal rules

tool_policy.surface: skill folders, references, scripts, examples, assets, optional hooks

rubric: The skill description is written for model triggering, not human summary.
Gotchas are grounded in real failures or repo-specific footguns.
References, scripts, and assets are progressively disclosed instead of frontloaded.

stop_conditions: The reusable skill or guidance has a clear trigger and gotchas.
A virtual or real task demonstrates the skill changes behavior.
Release harness passes if repository files changed.
human_escalation: The skill would encode team policy without owner review.
The skill needs credentials, destructive hooks, or production access.
